ocr.text: In Table 1, the analysis of the percent of damaged edges reveals critical performance metrics across various conditions. For
10% damaged edges, the AUC of GD is 0.852, the AUC of GA is 0.864, and the P-value is 1.80e-03. At 30% damaged edges,
the AUC of GD drops to 0.844, the AUC of GA is 0.860, with a P-value of 2.10e-03.
Percent of damaged edges  AUC of GD  AUC of GA  P-value
SAX  hh  hu  hud  ud  hh2  hu2
hh  49
hu  43
hud  2  52
ud  1  48
hh2  52
hu2  51
With 50% damaged edges, the AUC of GD further decreases to 0.816, and the AUC of GA is 0.836, accompanied by a P-value
of 1.91e-04. At 70% damaged edges, the AUC of GD is 0.760, the AUC of GA falls to 0.816, and the P-value is 2.38e-04.
Finally, for 90% damaged edges, the AUC of GD is 0.648, the AUC of GA is 0.784, and the P-value is 2.36e-05. Table 2
presents a summary of SAX data, where for hh, values are 49 in the hh column, with all other entries marked as —; for hu, it
shows 43, with others as —; the hud row contains 2 in hh and 52 in hu, while ud has 1 in hud and 48 in ud; hh2 has 52 in hh2,
and hu2 has 51 in hu2, with all other entries as —.
